basics.name: Yuzhen (Amy) Song
basics.label: Undergraduate Researcher in Robotics
basics.summary: Robotics Engineering undergrad at SUSTech with a focus on UAV Trajectory Planning, SLAM, and Sim-to-Real deployment. Actively seeking Fall 2027 Ph.D. opportunities.

work.name: Slope Center-of-Mass (CoM) Assistance System
highlights: Designed an experimental testbed for dynamic traction (200 N peak) with <5% force error.
Developed STM32 control for motor torque regulation; achieved 20-30% reduction in metabolic cost.

work.name: Autonomous UAV Mission Navigation (Fast-Planner)
highlights: Deployed Fast-Planner on ROS for real-time trajectory generation in randomized point-cloud environments.
Engineered custom circular benchmark tracks in RViz to validate obstacle avoidance.

work.name: ROS2-Based Mobile Manipulator (Multimodal HRI)
highlights: Architected a ROS2 mobile manipulator with SLAM and Nav2; integrated EKF for sensor fusion.
Developed HRI interface using MediaPipe for gestures and Vosk for offline voice commands.

work.name: Sim-to-Real Infrastructure for Dexterous Hand
highlights: Established robust simulation environments in NVIDIA Isaac Sim and Webots for RL training.
Optimized URDF models and kinematic constraints for high-fidelity physics simulation.

work.name: Autonomous Intelligent Rescue Vehicle
highlights: Won National Second Prize. Programmed STM32 FSM for chassis kinematics and servo grasping.
Trained YOLOv11 model for real-time multi-class recognition.

work.name: CV-Guided 6-DOF Robotic Sketching System
highlights: Developed OpenCV pipeline for sub-pixel contour extraction and solved 6-DOF IK via D-H parameters.

education.institution: Southern University of Science and Technology
courses: Core: ROS(100), Mechanics of Materials(100), Robot Modeling & Control(97), Mechanical Design(97), Control Engineering(90)

awards.title: National Second Prize
awards.awarder: China Undergraduate Eng. Practice and Innovation Ability Competition

awards.title: Provincial First Prize
awards.awarder: Guangdong Undergraduate Eng. Practice and Innovation Ability Competition
